# Question 1

## 1.1 Import datasets


``` r
library(readr)
price_data <- read_csv("3210007701_databaseLoadingData.csv")
```

```
## Rows: 6121 Columns: 15
## -- Column specification --------------------------------------------------------
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (8): REF_DATE, GEO, DGUID, Farm products, UOM, SCALAR_FACTOR, VECTOR, ST...
## dbl (5): UOM_ID, SCALAR_ID, COORDINATE, VALUE, DECIMALS
## lgl (2): SYMBOL, TERMINATED
## 
## i Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## i Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```

``` r
head(price_data)
```

```
## # A tibble: 6 x 15
##   REF_DATE GEO        DGUID `Farm products` UOM   UOM_ID SCALAR_FACTOR SCALAR_ID
##   <chr>    <chr>      <chr> <chr>           <chr>  <dbl> <chr>             <dbl>
## 1 2002-01  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## 2 2002-02  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## 3 2002-03  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## 4 2002-04  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## 5 2002-05  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## 6 2002-06  Prince Ed~ 2016~ Wheat (except ~ Doll~    117 units                 0
## # i 7 more variables: VECTOR <chr>, COORDINATE <dbl>, VALUE <dbl>,
## #   STATUS <chr>, SYMBOL <lgl>, TERMINATED <lgl>, DECIMALS <dbl>
```


``` r
data2 <- read_csv("3210035101_databaseLoadingData_2.csv")
```

```
## Rows: 288 Columns: 15
## -- Column specification --------------------------------------------------------
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (7): REF_DATE, GEO, DGUID, Type of grain, UOM, SCALAR_FACTOR, VECTOR
## dbl (5): UOM_ID, SCALAR_ID, COORDINATE, VALUE, DECIMALS
## lgl (3): STATUS, SYMBOL, TERMINATED
## 
## i Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## i Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```

``` r
head(data2)
```

```
## # A tibble: 6 x 15
##   REF_DATE GEO    DGUID     `Type of grain` UOM   UOM_ID SCALAR_FACTOR SCALAR_ID
##   <chr>    <chr>  <chr>     <chr>           <chr>  <dbl> <chr>             <dbl>
## 1 2002-01  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## 2 2002-02  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## 3 2002-03  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## 4 2002-04  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## 5 2002-05  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## 6 2002-06  Canada 2016A000~ Wheat, excludi~ Tonn~    288 units                 0
## # i 7 more variables: VECTOR <chr>, COORDINATE <dbl>, VALUE <dbl>,
## #   STATUS <lgl>, SYMBOL <lgl>, TERMINATED <lgl>, DECIMALS <dbl>
```

## 1.2 Graphical exploration (delivery data)


``` r
library(ggplot2)

delivery_data = data.frame(Date = data2$REF_DATE,
                           Value = as.numeric(data2$VALUE))

head(delivery_data)
```

```
##      Date   Value
## 1 2002-01 1101486
## 2 2002-02 1273057
## 3 2002-03 1079688
## 4 2002-04 1022353
## 5 2002-05  844496
## 6 2002-06  906858
```

``` r
p2 = ggplot(delivery_data, aes(x=Date, y = Value, group=1))+
             geom_line(color="blue", size=1)+
  labs(
    title = "Producer deliveries of Wheat, Excluding Durum",
    subtitle = "2002-2025",
    x = "Date (Monthly)",
    y = "Grain (Tonnes)",
    caption = "Source: Statistics Canada. Table 32-10-0351-01  Producer deliveries of major grains"
  ) 
```

```
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## i Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
```

``` r
print(p2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-3-1.pdf)<!-- -->

``` r
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)


#Downloading data
Data_Price <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stardustsunrise/Time_Series/refs/heads/main/3210007701_databaseLoadingData.csv")

head(Data_Price)
```

```
##   REF_DATE                  GEO       DGUID
## 1  2002-01 Prince Edward Island 2016A000211
```

``` r
str(Data_Price)
```

```
## 'data.frame':	6121 obs. of  15 variables:

##  $ DGUID        : chr  "2016A000211" "2016A000211" "2016A000211" "2016A000211" ...
##  $ Farm.products: chr  "Wheat (except durum wheat) [1121111]" "Wheat (except durum wheat) [1121111]" "Wheat (except durum wheat) [1121111]" "Wheat (except durum wheat) [1121111]" ...
```

``` r
print(Data_Price)
```

``` r
#Convert to data frame
Price_data = data.frame(Date= as.Date(paste0(Data_Price$REF_DATE,"-01")), 
                        Price_per_MTonne= Data_Price$VALUE,
                        Product=c("Wheat (except durum wheat) [1121111]"))
print(Price_data)
```

# Question 2

## 2.1 Removing the Trend (Delivery data)


``` r
delivery_data_D1 = diff(delivery_data$Value)
delivery_data_D2 = diff(delivery_data_D1)
delivery_data_D3 = diff(log(delivery_data$Value))

par(mfrow=c(2,2))

#Plot Original Graph
plot(delivery_data$Value, type = 'l', xlab = "Date", ylab = "Wheat (Tonnes)", main = "Original Data")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)

# First-Order Differencing
plot( delivery_data_D1, type = 'l', xlab = 'Date', ylab = "Wheat (Tonnes)", main = "First-order Differencing")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)

# Second-Order differencing
plot( delivery_data_D2, type = 'l', xlab = 'Date', ylab = "Wheat (Tonnes)", main = "Second-Order Differencing")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)

# Third-Order differencing
plot( delivery_data_D3, type = 'l', xlab = 'Date', ylab = "Wheat (Tonnes)", main = "Log Differencing")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-5-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#Log Differencing is the best option.
```

``` r
#converting time series to object

Price_data_ts=ts(Price_data$avg_price_canada,
                 start = c(2002-01-01,1),
                 frequency = 12)

str(Price_data_ts)
```

```
##  Time-Series [1:287] from 2000 to 2024: 209 211 212 204 209 ...
```

``` r
#calculate transformation
Diff=diff(Price_data_ts)#First difference
Log_diff=diff(log(Price_data_ts))#Difference of Logarithms

#create dataframe for visualisation
Price_data_transform=data.frame(
  Date=Price_data$Date[-1],
  Diff=as.numeric(Diff),
  Log_diff=as.numeric(Log_diff)
)

p1=ggplot(Price_data_transform,aes(x=Date,y=Diff))+
  geom_line(color="blue")+
  labs(title = "Wheat Price Data Set- First Difference",
       x="Date",
       y="Price per Ton")

p2=ggplot(Price_data_transform,aes(x=Date,y=Log_diff))+
  geom_line(color="green")+
  labs(title = "Wheat Price Data Set- Log Difference",
       x="Date",
       y="Price per Ton")

#display side by side
p1/p2
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-6-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
# preference for log difference
```

## 2.2 Removing Seasonal Effects (delivery data)


``` r
library(forecast)

acf(delivery_data_D3, 
    main="ACF of diff series",
    lag.max = 48,
    xlab = "Months",
    xlim=c(1,48)
  )
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-7-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#There are periodic spikes in the ACF graph at multiples of 12 months, so we will use lag=12 to remove the seasonality.

delivery_data_deseasonalized=diff(delivery_data_D3, lag=12)



plot( delivery_data_deseasonalized, type = 'l', xlab = 'Date', ylab = "Wheat (Tonnes)", main = "Deseasonalized")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-7-2.pdf)<!-- --> 

## 2.2 Removing seasonal component (price data)


``` r
library(forecast)

#Indentifying Seasonality

par(mfrow=c(1,2))
Acf(Diff,
main = "ACF of diff(Price of Wheat)",
lag.max = 300,
xlab = "Months", 
xlim=c(1,300))

Acf(Log_diff,
    main = "ACF of diff(log(Price of Wheat))",
    lag.max = 300,
    xlab = "Months", 
    xlim = c(1, 300))
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-8-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#Test for iid noise

# Ljung-Box tests
cat("1. Ljung-Box tests (H0: No autocorrelation up to lag 48):\n\n")
```

```
## 1. Ljung-Box tests (H0: No autocorrelation up to lag 48):
```

``` r
## 1. Ljung-Box tests (H0: No autocorrelation up to lag 48):

Box.test(Diff, lag = 12, type = "Ljung-Box")
```

```
## 
## 	Box-Ljung test
## 
## data:  Diff
## X-squared = 137.14, df = 12, p-value < 2.2e-16
```

``` r
Box.test(Diff, lag = 12, type = "Ljung-Box")
```

```
## 
## 	Box-Ljung test
## 
## data:  Diff
## X-squared = 137.14, df = 12, p-value < 2.2e-16
```

``` r
plot(Log_diff,type="l",xlab="n", ylab=expression("Log_diff"))
abline(h=0,col="darkgreen",lty=2)



# Removing Seasonality

P_D1<-diff(Log_diff,difference=1)
P_D3<-diff(Log_diff,differences=3)
P_D12<-diff(Log_diff,differences=12)
P_D24<-diff(Log_diff,differences=24)

# First order difference

plot( P_D1, type = 'l', xlab = 'Months' , ylab = expression(nabla*"Price Per Ton"))
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-8-2.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#third order difference
plot( P_D3, type = 'l', xlab = 'Months', ylab = expression(nabla^3*"Price Per Ton"))

#12th order difference
plot( P_D12, type = 'l', xlab = 'Months', ylab = expression(nabla^12*"Price Per Ton"))
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-8-3.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#24th order difference
plot( P_D24, type = 'l', xlab = 'Months', ylab = expression(nabla^24*"Price Per Ton"))


#preference to Difference of 1st order

Deseasonlised_pricedata=P_D1
#2.3 Analysis of differenced Series (Price Data)
library(ggplot2)
Mean_pricedata=mean(Deseasonlised_pricedata)

Mean_pricedata
```

```
## [1] -2.277604e-05
```

``` r
Variance_pricedata=var(Deseasonlised_pricedata)

Variance_pricedata
```


``` r
Variance_pricedata
```



```
##       Min.    1st Qu.     Median       Mean    3rd Qu.       Max.

```

``` r
lower = qnorm(mean = Mean_pricedata, sd = Standard_dav_Pricedata, p=0.05)

upper = qnorm(mean = Mean_pricedata, sd = Standard_dav_Pricedata, p=0.95)

plot(Deseasonlised_pricedata, type = 'l', main = 'Price Data, deseasonalized', xlab = 'month', ylab = "Wheat shipped, tonnes")
abline(h = 0, col = 'darkblue', lty = 2)
abline(h = lower, col = 'darkgreen', lty = 2)
abline(h = upper, col = 'darkred', lty = 2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-8-4.pdf)<!-- --> 

``` r
#Check if 95% is within.
number=0
for(n in 1:length(Deseasonlised_pricedata)){
  if(Deseasonlised_pricedata[n]>upper){
    number=number+1
  }
  if(Deseasonlised_pricedata[n]<lower){
    number=number+1
  }
}
 number
```

```
## [1] 29
```

``` r
 #29 out of 287 (about 10%) of the values lie outside this range. If the process is stationary, about 5% (14.35) should lie outside this interval on average.
 
 acf(Deseasonlised_pricedata, 
    main="ACF of deseasonalized series",
    lag.max = 300,
    xlab = "Months",
    xlim=c(1,12)
  )
```

## 2.3 Analysis of differenced Series (delivery data)


``` r
mean=mean(delivery_data_deseasonalized)
varience = var(delivery_data_deseasonalized)

std_dev = sqrt(varience)

lower = qnorm(mean = mean, sd = std_dev, p=0.05)
upper = qnorm(mean = mean, sd = std_dev, p=0.95)

plot(delivery_data_deseasonalized, type = 'l', main = 'Delivery data, deseasonalized', xlab = 'month', ylab = "Wheat shipped, tonnes")
abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)
abline(h = lower, col = 'red', lty = 2)
abline(h = upper, col = 'red', lty = 2)
```

``` r
#Check if 95% is within.
number=0
for(n in 1:length(delivery_data_deseasonalized)){
  if(delivery_data_deseasonalized[n]>upper){
    number=number+1
  }
  if(delivery_data_deseasonalized[n]<lower){
    number=number+1
  }
}
```

```
## [1] 26
```

``` r
 #26 out of 275 (about 9%) of the values lie outside this range. If the process is stationary, about 5% (13.75) should lie outside this interval on average.
 
 acf(delivery_data_deseasonalized, 
    main="ACF of deseasonalized series",
    lag.max = 48,
    xlab = "Months",
    xlim=c(1,48)
  )
```


# Question 3

## 3.1 Linear Filter (delivery data)

We have $$X̃_t = (1/(2q+1)) Σ_{j=-q}^{q} X_{t-j}$$


``` r
delivery_ts <- ts(data2$VALUE,
                  start = c(2002,1), # Start year and month: January 2002
                  frequency = 12) # 12 observations per year (monthly data)


# Define moving average filter function
moving_average <- function(X, q) {
  N <- length(X)              # Length of input series
  weight <- 1 / (2 * q + 1)   # Equal weights for symmetric MA
  indices <- (q+1):(N-q)      # Indices where filter can be applied
  X_filt <- rep(NA, N)        # Initialize output with NAs
  
  # Apply moving average filter
  for (i in indices) {
    X_filt[i] <- sum(X[(i-q):(i+q)] * weight)
  }
  return(X_filt)               # Return filtered series
}

# Apply moving average to your existing time series



X_filt1 <- ts(moving_average(delivery_ts, 6),
              start = start(delivery_ts),
              frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))

X_filt2 <- ts(moving_average(delivery_ts, 12),
              start = start(delivery_ts),
              frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))


zoom_start <- 180
zoom_end <- 288



delivery_zoom <- window(delivery_ts,
                        start = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_start],
                        end = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_end])
X_filt1_zoom <- window(X_filt1,
                       start = time(X_filt1)[zoom_start],

                       start = time(X_filt2)[zoom_start],
                       end = time(X_filt2)[zoom_end])
```

## 3.1 Linear Filter (price data)

We have $$X̃_t = (1/(2q+1)) Σ_{j=-q}^{q} X_{t-j}$$


``` r
Series_to_filter <- Price_data_ts
n <- length(Series_to_filter)

# --- q = 6 ---
q1 <- 6 #this is in the range from 5 to 9, helping reduce the high volatility of the data
weights1 <- rep(1/(2*q1+1), 2*q1+1)

Linear_filtered_q6 <- stats::filter(
  Series_to_filter,
  filter = weights1,
  sides = 2
)

# --- q = 12 ---
q2 <- 12 #this helps to address the seasonality of the data
weights2 <- rep(1/(2*q2+1), 2*q2+1)

Linear_filtered_q12 <- stats::filter(
  Series_to_filter,
  filter = weights2,
  sides = 2
)

# --- Plot ---
plot(Series_to_filter, type="l", col="black",
     main="Linear Moving Average Filter Price Data",
     ylab="Values")

lines(Linear_filtered_q6, col="blue", lwd=2)
lines(Linear_filtered_q12, col="red", lwd=2)

legend("topright",
       legend=c("Original", "q = 6", "q = 12"),
       col=c("black", "blue", "red"),
       lty=1, lwd=c(1,2,2))
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-11-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

Justification of q = 12: Monthly data → annual seasonal structure ≈ 12 months. Justification of q = 6: The data shows a decently high level of volatility (or high variance in other terms)

## 3.2 Exponential filter (delivery data)


``` r
exponential_smoothing <- function(X, alpha) {
  N <- length(X)
  X_smooth <- rep(NA, N)
  
  # Initialize with first observation
  X_smooth[1] <- X[1]
  
  # Recursive smoothing
  for (t in 2:N) {
    X_smooth[t] <- alpha * X[t] + (1 - alpha) * X_smooth[t - 1]
  }
  
  return(X_smooth)
}



X_exp1 <- ts(exponential_smoothing(delivery_ts, alpha = 0.1),
             start = start(delivery_ts),
             frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))

X_exp2 <- ts(exponential_smoothing(delivery_ts, alpha = 0.2),
             start = start(delivery_ts),
             frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))



zoom_start <- 50
zoom_end <- 200

delivery_zoom <- window(delivery_ts,
                        start = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_start],
                        end = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_end])


X_exp1_zoom <- window(X_exp1,
                      start = time(X_exp1)[zoom_start],
                      end = time(X_exp1)[zoom_end])
X_exp2_zoom <- window(X_exp2, 
                      start = time(X_exp2)[zoom_start],
                      end = time(X_exp2)[zoom_end])





plot.ts(delivery_zoom,
        main = 'Delivery Data (Exponential Smoothing Zoom)',
        xlab = 'Time',
        ylab = 'Values')

lines(X_exp1_zoom, col = 'red', lwd = 2)
lines(X_exp2_zoom, col = 'blue', lwd = 2)

abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Original", "alpha = 0.1", "alpha = 0.3"),
       col = c('black', 'red', 'blue'),
       lty = 1,
       lwd = 2)
```

## 3.2 Exponential filter (price data)


``` r
exponential_smoothing <- function(X, alpha) {
  N <- length(X)
  X_smooth <- rep(NA, N)
  
  # Initialize with first observation
  X_smooth[1] <- X[1]
  
  # Recursive smoothing
  for (t in 2:N) {
    X_smooth[t] <- alpha * X[t] + (1 - alpha) * X_smooth[t - 1]
  }
  
  return(X_smooth)
}

# --- Alpha for seasonal smoothing ---
X_exp1 <- ts(exponential_smoothing(Price_data_ts, alpha = 0.2),
             start = start(Price_data_ts),
             frequency = frequency(Price_data_ts))
# --- Alpha for high volatility ---
X_exp2 <- ts(exponential_smoothing(Price_data_ts, alpha = 0.8),
             start = start(Price_data_ts),
             frequency = frequency(Price_data_ts))
plot.ts(Price_data_ts,
        main = 'Exponential Smoothing Comparison Price Data',
        xlab = 'Time',
        ylab = 'Values')

lines(X_exp1, col = 'red')
lines(X_exp2, col = 'blue')

abline(h = 0, col = 'black', lty = 2)

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Original", "alpha = 0.2", "alpha = 0.8"),
       col = c('black', 'red', 'blue'),
       lty = 1,
       lwd = 2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-13-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

I would recommend the linear filter since the Exponential one does not appear to make as big of a difference. The optimal filtering from the ones chosen is linear with q=12 as it appears to be the most smooth from those seen

# 3.3 Comparison of Filtering Techniques


``` r
q_choice <- 12
alpha_choice <- 0.1


# Moving average (linear smoothing)
X_ma <- ts(moving_average(delivery_ts, q_choice),
           start = start(delivery_ts),
           frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))

# Exponential smoothing
X_exp <- ts(exponential_smoothing(delivery_ts, alpha_choice),
            start = start(delivery_ts),
            frequency = frequency(delivery_ts))


zoom_start = 180
zoom_end <- 288

delivery_zoom <- window(delivery_ts,
                        start = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_start],
                        end = time(delivery_ts)[zoom_end])

                    start = time(X_ma)[zoom_start],
                    end = time(X_ma)[zoom_end])

                     start = time(X_exp)[zoom_start],
                     end = time(X_exp)[zoom_end])

#plot with zoom

        main = paste("MA(q =", q_choice, ") vs Exp(alpha =", alpha_choice, ")"),
        xlab = "Time",

lines(X_exp_zoom, col = "blue", lwd = 2)

abline(h = 0, col = "black", lty = 2)

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Original",
                  paste("MA q =", q_choice),
```

# Question 4

## 4.1 Construction of Stationary Series (Delivery Data)


``` r
residuals_q12 <- delivery_ts - X_filt2

#autocorr function

autocorr <- function(X, hmax) {
  
  X <- na.omit(X)     # Remove NA values
  N <- length(X)
  hmax <- min(hmax, N - 1)
  
  mu <- mean(X)
  gamma0 <- sum((X - mu)^2) / N   # Variance at lag 0
  
  acf_values <- numeric(hmax + 1)
  
  for (h in 0:hmax) {
    gamma_h <- sum((X[1:(N-h)] - mu) * (X[(h+1):N] - mu)) / N
    acf_values[h + 1] <- gamma_h / gamma0
  }
  
  return(acf_values)
}




# Detrend the series by subtracting the moving average (q = 12)
residuals_q12 <- delivery_ts - X_filt2   # Residuals after removing trend/seasonality

# Set up 1x2 plot layout
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Panel 1: Plot the detrended series
plot.ts(na.omit(residuals_q12),
        main = "Detrended series (q = 12)",
        ylab = "Residuals",
        xlab = "Time")

abline(h = 0, col = "red")

# Panel 2: ACF of detrended series
hmax <- 20  # Maximum lag for ACF

acf1 <- autocorr(na.omit(residuals_q12), hmax)  # Compute ACF 



#Nyk's code
res <- na.omit(residuals_q12)

mu <- mean(res)
sigma <- sd(res)

upper <- mu + 1.96 * sigma
lower <- mu - 1.96 * sigma


number <- 0

for (n in 1:length(residuals_q12)) {
  
  x <- residuals_q12[n]
  
  if (!is.na(x)) {   # Skip NA values
    
    if (x > upper) {
      number <- number + 1
    }
    
    if (x < lower) {
      number <- number + 1
    }
  }
}

number
```

```
## [1] 15
```

## Significance Test (Delivery Data)


``` r
Stationary_series <- Deseasonlised_pricedata
library(tseries)

# ADF Test
adf.test(Stationary_series)
```

```
## Warning in adf.test(Stationary_series): p-value smaller than printed p-value
```

```
## 
## 	Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  Stationary_series
## Dickey-Fuller = -8.908, Lag order = 6, p-value = 0.01
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
```

``` r
# KPSS Test
kpss.test(Stationary_series)
```

```
## Warning in kpss.test(Stationary_series): p-value greater than printed p-value
```

```
## 
## 	KPSS Test for Level Stationarity
## 
## data:  Stationary_series
## KPSS Level = 0.011462, Truncation lag parameter = 5, p-value = 0.1
```

## 4.1 Construction of Stationary Series (Price)

For the price data the deseasonalized series was chosen since it does not appear to have a trend or a major seasonal component, whereas the filtered data appears to still have a trend. Now we can test for stationarity


``` r
plot(Deseasonlised_pricedata, type = 'l', main = 'Price Data, deseasonalized', xlab = 'month', ylab = "Wheat shipped, tonnes")
abline(h = 0, col = 'darkblue', lty = 2)
abline(h = lower, col = 'darkgreen', lty = 2)
abline(h = upper, col = 'darkred', lty = 2)
```

## 4.2 Autocorrelation Function (ACF) $$ρ^​(h)=∑t=1n​(Xt​−Xˉ)2∑t=h+1n​(Xt​−Xˉ)(Xt−h​−Xˉ)​$$

## Delivery Data


``` r
ACF_estimator <- function(X, hmax){
  n <- length(X)
  X_bar <- mean(X)
  acf_vals <- numeric(hmax+1)
  
  for(h in 0:hmax){
    num <- sum((X[(h+1):n]-X_bar)*
                 (X[1:(n-h)]-X_bar))
    den <- sum((X-X_bar)^2)
    acf_vals[h+1] <- num/den
  }
  
  return(acf_vals)
}


res <- na.omit(residuals_q12)

hmax <- 40
acf_values <- ACF_estimator(res, hmax)

CI <- 1.96 / sqrt(length(res))

plot(0:hmax, acf_values, type = "h",
     main = "Estimated ACF",
     xlab = "Lag", ylab = "ACF")
abline(h = 0)
abline(h = CI, col = "red", lty = 2)
abline(h = -CI, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-19-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

## Price Data


``` r
hmax <- 40
acf_values <- ACF_estimator(Stationary_series, hmax)

# 95% CI
CI <- 1.96/sqrt(length(Stationary_series))

plot(0:hmax, acf_values, type="h",
     main="Estimated ACF",
     xlab="Lag", ylab="ACF")
abline(h=0)
abline(h=CI, col="red", lty=2)
abline(h=-CI, col="red", lty=2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-20-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

We have some seasonal spikes, which means there is remaining periodicity.

## 4.3 Custom PACF Function

## Delivery Data


``` r
PACF_estimator <- function(X, hmax){
  
  X <- na.omit(X)        # ← critical line
  pacf_vals <- numeric(hmax)
  
  for(h in 1:hmax){
    model <- ar(X, order.max = h, aic = FALSE, method = "yw")
    pacf_vals[h] <- model$ar[h]
  }
  
  return(pacf_vals)
}


pacf_values <- PACF_estimator(residuals_q12, hmax)

plot(1:hmax, pacf_values, type="h",
     main="Estimated PACF",
     xlab="Lag", ylab="PACF")
abline(h=0)
abline(h=CI, col="blue", lty=2)
abline(h=-CI, col="blue", lty=2)
```

![](Time-Series-Project_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-21-1.pdf)<!-- --> 

## Price Data


``` r
pacf_values <- PACF_estimator(Stationary_series, hmax)

plot(1:hmax, pacf_values, type="h",
     main="Estimated PACF",
     xlab="Lag", ylab="PACF")
abline(h=0)
abline(h=CI, col="blue", lty=2)
abline(h=-CI, col="blue", lty=2)
```
